steps.title: Find the foreign-versus-local contrast
steps.content: The most telling detail is the explicit pairing of activism for “the exile from far-distant shores” with the call to “remember” that “men are still wasting life's crimson around your own doors.”

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice D is the speaker directly criticizing activists (“ye plead”) by reminding them that while they focus on distant exiles, people suffer at home—the exact hypocrisy described in the claim. A, B, and C don't capture this selective attention to foreign causes over local ones. That matches choice D — D is correct.
